meta_title: Roofing in South Palm Beach, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in South Palm Beach, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimate!
h1: South Palm Beach's Trusted Roofing Experts — Built for the Coast, Built to Last
intro:
South Palm Beach is one of Palm Beach County's most coveted coastal communities, where homes in neighborhoods like Palm Beach Hampton and Barclay sit mere steps from the Atlantic Ocean and carry average values near $450,000. With that kind of investment resting beneath a single roofline, the quality of your roof isn't just an aesthetic decision — it's a financial and safety imperative. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand exactly what it takes to protect a home this close to the water, and we bring that expertise to every project we undertake in this unique coastal town.

The oceanfront exposure that makes South Palm Beach so desirable also makes it one of the most demanding environments for roofing systems in all of Florida. Sitting just 0.1 miles from the coast, homes here face salt-laden air year-round that accelerates material degradation, intense UV exposure that breaks down standard roofing materials faster than inland properties, and a 170 mph design wind speed designation that demands nothing short of the most robust hurricane-rated products available. For the 65% of South Palm Beach homes built before 1994 — constructed to standards that predate modern Florida Building Code hurricane requirements — the roof above your head may be the single greatest vulnerability your property faces.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed more than 500 roofing projects across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we know that South Palm Beach homeowners expect both performance and craftsmanship. Whether you're looking at a full roof replacement on a pre-code home or targeted storm damage repairs after years of coastal exposure, our team brings 60+ years of combined experience directly to your door. We handle everything from permitting through the Town of South Palm Beach Building Department to final inspection — with zero subcontractors, zero shortcuts, and a commitment to service that has earned us an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau.

section_why.h2: Why South Palm Beach Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in South Palm Beach isn't a decision to take lightly. The coastal conditions here demand a company that doesn't just sell roofing products — but one that truly understands how wind, salt, humidity, and hurricane season interact with a home's roofing system over time. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing was built for exactly this environment. As 'The Kings of Service,' we've staked our reputation on delivering roofing installations that hold up year after year, project after project, storm after storm.

One of the things that sets us apart most significantly is our 100% in-house installation policy. Every roofer who shows up at your South Palm Beach home is a trained, vetted Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee — not a day-labor subcontractor hired off the street. This matters enormously when it comes to quality control, accountability, and the precision required to meet Palm Beach County's strict hurricane-rated roofing codes. Our crews are experienced with the specific challenges that coastal properties present: proper flashing and underlayment at roof edges and intersections most vulnerable to wind-driven rain, deck preparation on aging pre-1994 structures, and correct fastening patterns required for 170 mph wind zone compliance.

With over 500 homes completed across South Florida, our team has handled virtually every roofing scenario imaginable — from historic tile roofs on Mediterranean-style homes along the Barclay stretch to architectural shingle replacements on modern construction near South Palm Beach Town Hall. We work closely with the Town of South Palm Beach Building Department from permit application through final inspection, ensuring that every project we complete is fully code-compliant and properly documented for your insurance carrier and future home sale.

We also offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, because we believe that protecting a high-value coastal home should never be out of reach. When you combine our licensing, insurance, A+ BBB rating, and decades of local experience, the choice becomes clear — Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the roofing company South Palm Beach trusts.

section_history.h2: Storm History That Proves Why South Palm Beach Roofs Can't Afford to Fail
section_history.body:
South Palm Beach has weathered more than eight named storms since 1990, and three of those storms left lasting marks on the community that residents still reference today. Hurricane Frances made landfall in 2004 as a powerful Category 2 storm, delivering sustained winds that tore through aging rooflines throughout Palm Beach County. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma — one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded — swept across South Florida in 2005, producing catastrophic wind damage that overwhelmed thousands of homes that had already been weakened by Frances. The one-two punch of back-to-back major hurricanes in consecutive seasons fundamentally changed how South Palm Beach homeowners think about roof longevity and storm readiness.

Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017. While Irma made landfall on the southwest coast of Florida, its sprawling wind field extended far enough to deliver tropical storm and near-hurricane-force gusts to coastal Palm Beach County communities. Homes in neighborhoods like Palm Beach Hampton saw shingle blow-off, ridge cap failures, and roof-to-wall connection damage — particularly in pre-1994 structures that lacked modern hurricane strapping and enhanced fastening patterns. These storm events aren't ancient history; they're a clear preview of what South Palm Beach will face again as Atlantic hurricane seasons continue to intensify.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we use every one of these storms as a case study in what fails — and why. The lessons from Frances, Wilma, and Irma are built directly into how we specify roofing systems, select underlayments, and determine fastening schedules for every South Palm Beach home we service. A properly installed, hurricane-rated roof isn't just about surviving the next storm — it's about giving you peace of mind for every storm season to come.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in South Palm Beach
section_code.body:
South Palm Beach falls within Palm Beach County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code, 8th Edition, and while the town sits just outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — which applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — it is absolutely not exempt from rigorous hurricane-rated requirements. The town's 170 mph design wind speed designation means that every roofing product installed in South Palm Beach must meet stringent performance standards for wind uplift resistance, impact resistance, and proper attachment to the structural deck below.

All roofing permits in South Palm Beach must be pulled through the Town of South Palm Beach Building Department. This process includes submission of product approval documentation confirming that all shingles, tiles, underlayments, and fasteners meet Florida Product Approval (FPA) standards for the applicable wind zone. For homes built before 1994 — which make up approximately 65% of South Palm Beach's housing stock — the building department may also require deck inspections and structural assessments to ensure that the existing sheathing can support modern code-compliant attachment patterns. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every aspect of this permitting process on your behalf, so you never have to navigate the bureaucracy alone.

For tile roofing systems specifically, Palm Beach County requires adherence to strict mortar-set or mechanically fastened installation methods that have been tested and approved for the area's wind design loads. For shingle roofing, high-nailing patterns and enhanced underlayment systems like peel-and-stick self-adhering membranes are standard requirements rather than upgrades. Our team is fully versed in these requirements and builds them into every installation by default — not as add-ons.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems for South Palm Beach
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've carefully selected our roofing product lines to meet the specific demands of coastal South Florida — and the two brands we install in South Palm Beach represent the best that the industry has to offer. For architectural shingle roofing, we exclusively install CertainTeed's Landmark Series, a premium line of laminated architectural shingles that combines exceptional wind resistance with outstanding curb appeal. The Landmark Series carries a Class A fire rating, a limited lifetime warranty, and wind resistance ratings up to 130 mph for standard installation — with enhanced ratings available through CertainTeed's SureStart PLUS warranty program when installed by certified contractors like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing.

For homeowners seeking the classic Florida aesthetic and superior longevity of tile roofing, we install West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. West Lake tiles are manufactured to meet Florida Product Approval standards and are specifically engineered for the high-wind coastal environments found in communities like South Palm Beach. Concrete tile offers exceptional durability, energy efficiency from its thermal mass properties, and a wide range of profiles and colors that complement the Mediterranean and contemporary architectural styles common throughout Palm Beach Hampton and Barclay. Clay tile, while a premium option, offers unmatched longevity — properly installed clay tile systems routinely outlast the structures beneath them.

Both CertainTeed and West Lake products are installed over premium underlayment systems that provide a critical secondary water barrier — an especially important feature for a town as coastal as South Palm Beach, where wind-driven rain during tropical events can force water under almost any roofing surface. Our underlayment specifications always meet or exceed what Palm Beach County code requires, providing an extra layer of protection between your home and the storm.

section_cost.h2: Roofing Costs in South Palm Beach: What to Expect and How to Finance It
section_cost.body:
Roofing costs in South Palm Beach vary based on several factors: the size and pitch of your roof, the material you select, the condition of your existing deck, and the complexity of your roofline. For a typical single-family home in the 33480 ZIP code — which tends to fall in the 2,000 to 3,000 square foot range — a full architectural shingle replacement with premium underlayment and code-compliant fastening generally runs between $15,000 and $22,000. Tile roofing systems, given the higher material and labor costs associated with concrete and clay installation, typically range from $22,000 to $40,000 or more depending on tile selection and structural considerations. For a town where average home values sit near $450,000, investing in a quality roof is one of the highest-ROI improvements you can make.

To make premium roofing accessible for every South Palm Beach homeowner, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available on approved credit. Our financing programs are designed to let you get your roof replaced now — before the next storm season — without waiting until you've saved the full amount. When you factor in the insurance savings that come with a new hurricane-rated roof (discussed in the next section), many homeowners find that the financing payment is partially or fully offset by the reduction in their annual premium.

Beyond the immediate cost, consider the long-term financial picture: a new CertainTeed Landmark Series roof or West Lake tile system adds measurable value to your home at resale, reduces the likelihood of costly interior damage from water intrusion, and may extend the life of your HVAC system by improving attic ventilation and thermal performance. In South Palm Beach's competitive real estate market, a new roof is one of the strongest selling points you can offer a prospective buyer.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for South Palm Beach Homeowners Who Upgrade Their Roof
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for replacing an aging roof in South Palm Beach is the insurance premium savings that typically follow. Florida's property insurance market has been notoriously volatile in recent years, with carriers exiting the state or dramatically increasing premiums — particularly for coastal communities in Palm Beach County where storm risk is statistically higher. However, homeowners who can demonstrate a new, hurricane-rated roof to their insurer often see significant relief in the form of wind mitigation credits.

After a new roof installation, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing strongly recommends scheduling a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector. The resulting wind mitigation report documents key features of your new roof — including the roof covering type and approval, deck attachment method, roof-to-wall connection type, and secondary water resistance measures — and is submitted directly to your insurance carrier. In many cases, South Palm Beach homeowners with new CertainTeed or West Lake roofing systems installed to current code standards have seen annual premium reductions of $1,000 to $3,000 or more depending on their carrier and coverage level. Over a 10-year period, these savings can represent a substantial offset against the cost of the roof itself.

Major carriers operating in Palm Beach County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and several private market insurers — all recognize wind mitigation credits as defined by Florida law. Some carriers also offer discounts for new roof age alone, since a roof installed within the past 5 years dramatically reduces the statistical likelihood of a major claim. If your current roof is approaching 15 to 20 years old, your insurer may already be threatening non-renewal — a new roof from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can resolve that issue immediately while simultaneously lowering your premiums.

faqs.q: How do I know if my South Palm Beach home needs a full roof replacement or just repairs?
faqs.a: The answer depends on the age, condition, and extent of damage to your existing roof. Homes built before 1994 — which make up about 65% of South Palm Beach's housing stock — often have roofing systems that have exceeded their intended lifespan and may not meet current code standards. If your roof is 20+ years old, has widespread granule loss, multiple areas of lifted or missing shingles, or has experienced repeated leaks, a full replacement is usually the more cost-effective long-term solution. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free assessments to help you make that determination.

faqs.q: How much does a new roof cost in South Palm Beach, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a typical South Palm Beach home, architectural shingle roof replacements generally range from $15,000 to $22,000, while concrete or clay tile systems typically range from $22,000 to $40,000+. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options on approved credit, making it possible to get a new hurricane-rated roof before storm season without a large upfront payment. Contact us for a free, detailed estimate tailored to your specific home.

faqs.q: What permits are required for a roof replacement in South Palm Beach, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: All roofing replacements in South Palm Beach require a permit through the Town of South Palm Beach Building Department. The permit process involves submission of product approval documentation confirming Florida Building Code compliance, a review of the existing deck condition for pre-1994 homes, and a final inspection upon completion. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every step of this process on your behalf — from permit application to final sign-off — so you can focus on everything else while we manage the paperwork.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in South Palm Beach?
faqs.a: Most single-family residential roof replacements in South Palm Beach are completed within 1 to 3 days for shingle systems, depending on roof size, pitch, and complexity. Tile systems may take 3 to 5 days due to the additional labor involved in setting concrete or clay tile. Permit processing time through the Town of South Palm Beach Building Department typically adds 5 to 10 business days to the overall project timeline. We always aim to minimize disruption and keep you informed every step of the way.

faqs.q: Will a new roof lower my homeowners insurance in South Palm Beach?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases significantly. After your new roof is installed, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document its features — including roof covering type, deck attachment, and secondary water resistance — for submission to your insurance carrier. South Palm Beach homeowners with new hurricane-rated roofs have seen annual premium reductions of $1,000 to $3,000 or more depending on their carrier and coverage. Additionally, some carriers in Palm Beach County will not renew policies on homes with older roofs, so a new installation can also help you maintain continuous coverage.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your South Palm Beach Home? Get Your Free Roofing Estimate Today.
cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is standing by to assess your South Palm Beach roof, answer every question you have, and deliver a no-obligation estimate — because your home deserves the Kings of Service. Call us today or request your free quote online.
